The AI advancements, from ChatGPT to autogenerative tools in Adobe programs. Has really help speed up tasks that use to take a lot longer.
Smartphones! I'm a teacher, so the ability to have knowledge right into the palm of someone's hand is amazing. If you know how to look, any question you have can be answered. Plus, silly snail game!

Xennial here - there has been so much in the last 25 years I don't know that I could pick one. From high-speed internet vs. dial-up, flat-panel tvs vs. b&w tube behemoths, smart phones vs. land lines, mRNA vaccines, EVs and battery technology, now LLMs and quantum computing. I feel like Hob in The Sandman - it's all so amazing.
Smart phones in general. Shows just how good technology can be and it also exposes the dumb and terrible people that use it too
Bluetooth technologies for reasons I will not disclose
those 3D printers that are making it possible to print organs they are really really cool
Gotta be smartphones! If you remember having a giant brick of a phone its a world away from the technological freedom we have nowadays
Auto-save, because no one else is coming to save me from leaving 20 important documents open until my computer crashes

For me the biggest change has been how we can communicate with each other. The fact that 20-30 years ago it was expensive calls and expensive text messages, and now we have endless cheap data and internet to have quick contact with anyone around the world for almost no cost at all!
